repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest repo entries pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 and pid_long_term_surrogate_v1 use different targets

published_prior_art.cite: FrangakisRubin2002 defines principal strata; the displayed interval is routine binary mixture algebra

repo_prior_art.cite: none; pid_long_term_surrogate_v1 is long-term prevalence-drift algebra, not responder CEP non-equivalence

published_prior_art.cite: MaYinLiuGeng2021 shows individual surrogate paradoxes can survive average criteria; YinLiuGengLuo2019 gives average exclusion criteria

repo_prior_art.cite: none; pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 studies ordinal K>=3 principal-score compatibility, not binary responder active faces

published_prior_art.cite: Kuroki2013 Stat Med gives sharp surrogate-endpoint bounds, but the proposal's KurokiCai2013 Biometrics citation could not be verified

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: KurokiCai2013 is not a verified Biometrics 2013 surrogate-endpoint paper; the verified surrogate sharp-bounds paper is Kuroki 2013 in Statistics in Medicine, while Kuroki-Cai is a UAI 2008 unobserved-variable bounds paper.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship generic open-subset claim has no concrete non-corner witness verified to lie in the Yin-Liu-Geng-Luo average exclusion region; Exhibit 9.1 computes concordance but not E_avg membership.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline promises a computable witness polytope and active-face certificate with nonempty relative interior, but Exhibit 9.1 gives only one endpoint table and does not compute the polytope or an interior check from primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The comparison to KurokiCai2013 is inaccurate: the cited Biometrics 69(1):76-84 surrogate-endpoint paper could not be verified, and the closest verified surrogate sharp-bounds source is Kuroki 2013 Statistics in Medicine.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The tier is field below novelty_target=flagship unless the draft separates its responder-cell frontier from MaYinLiuGeng2021 and the Chen/Ju/VanderWeele consistent-surrogate line with an explicit verified E_avg witness.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: In Exhibit 9.1 the implied average endpoint effect is 0.30*(0.30-0.10)+0.40*(0.20-0.70)+0.30*(0.90-0.80)=-0.11, so the worked table is itself an average surrogate-paradox law rather than a sanity check for average-paradox exclusion plus responder-cell harm.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Exhibit 9.1 with a table that explicitly computes all Yin-Liu-Geng-Luo exclusion inequalities and has positive average endpoint effect while theta_R<0.
Compute W_R(P) as a small finite polytope in the exhibit: list variables, active constraints, one relative-interior point, and the two endpoint-attaining faces.
Fix the Kuroki citation and add Ju-Geng 2010 or another consistent-surrogate comparator if it is closer to the average-exclusion side of the frontier.

recommended_next_step: Revise around a verified E_avg witness and a computed witness-polytope exhibit; without that, the kernel remains field-tier and cannot meet the flagship floor.
